tokens.range: gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used (in the plural thus, especially with the article) of the supreme God; occasionally applied by way of deference to magistrates; and sometimes as a supe
literally firmness; figuratively security; morally fidelity
cessation, i.e. an end (especially of the earth); often used adverb, no further; also the ankle (in the dual), as being the extremity of the leg or foot
the earth (at large, or partitively a land)
a house (in the greatest variation of applications, especially family, etc.)
properly, to mark (so as to be recognized), i.e. to remember; by implication, to mention;  to be male
kindness; by implication (towards God) piety; rarely (by opposition) reproof, or (subject.) beauty
something saved, i.e. (abstractly) deliverance; hence, aid, victory, prosperity
Jisrael, a symbolical name of Jacob; also (typically) of his posterity
to see, literally or figuratively (in numerous applications, direct and implied, transitive, intransitive and causative)

flags.note: אֱלֹהִים — Grammatically plural, normally takes singular verbs when referring to Israel's God. Translating as "God" (singular) interprets the plural; "gods" would be technically accurate but theologically wrong in monotheistic contexts.
חֶסֶד — No English equivalent. Combines covenant loyalty, active kindness, and steadfast love. "Mercy" is too weak; "lovingkindness" is archaic; "steadfast love" misses the relational dimension.

3.struct: contains a main verb; ⚑ theologically contested vocabulary

tokens.range: the earth (at large, or partitively a land)
play upon it; to make music, accompanied by the voice; hence to celebrate in song and music
Jehovah, Jewish national name of God
to break out (in joyful sound)
to mar (especially by breaking); figuratively, to split the ears (with sound), i.e. shout (for alarm or joy)
properly, to creak (or emit a stridulous sound), i.e. to shout (usually for joy)

flags.note: יהוה — The divine name (YHWH). LORD (small caps) hides the personal name. Yahweh/Jehovah are reconstructions of the consonants. The MKT's choice shapes the reader's sense of intimacy with God.

the face (as the part that turns); used in a great variety of applications (literally and figuratively); also (with prepositional prefix) as a preposition (before, etc.)
the right (natural, moral or legal); also (abstractly) equity or (figuratively) prosperity
to judge, i.e. pronounce sentence (for or against); by implication, to vindicate or punish; by extenssion, to govern; passively, to litigate (literally or figuratively)

Jehovah, Jewish national name of God
something saved, i.e. (abstractly) deliverance; hence, aid, victory, prosperity
an eye (literally or figuratively); by analogy, a fountain (as the eye of the landscape)
rightness (abstractly), subjectively (rectitude), objectively (justice), morally (virtue) or figuratively (prosperity)

flags.note: Dispute level 2: multiple significant translation options.
יהוה — The divine name (YHWH). LORD (small caps) hides the personal name. Yahweh/Jehovah are reconstructions of the consonants. The MKT's choice shapes the reader's sense of intimacy with God.
צְדָקָה — "righteousness" (moral quality) vs. "justice" (social-legal action). OT prophets use this for God's vindicating action for the oppressed; "righteousness" alone misses the social dimension.
